title: 📜 1. The “Indulgence Booth” (Joke Section)
content:
📜 1. The “Indulgence Booth” (Joke Section)

📜 Renamed: “The Pardoner’s Purge: Buy Your Way to Redemption (Totally Not a Scam!)”
✔ Students can attempt to “pay” (XP or an assignment task) to have their negative effects removed.
✔ Response: They get a ridiculous, clearly fraudulent reply from the Pardoner character, e.g.:
• “Ah yes, dear scholar, for the mere price of 50 XP, your sins are absolved! But wait…your new sin is believing this would actually work.”
• “Oh, you wish to remove Writer’s Block? Very well, I shall summon the ghost of Shakespeare! He says… ‘Just write, dummy.’”

Three-Layered Reference
1. Meta-Layer: The absurdity of modern transactional education & online paywalls.
2. Literary Layer: Ties into Chaucer’s Canterbury Tales and the idea of indulgences.
3. Weekly Theme Layer: Will change wording based on the theme (e.g., for Gothic Week, the “Pardoner” might instead be a demonic trickster offering false salvation).

✅ Why This Works:
• Gives students an in-game joke interaction that reinforces literary themes.
context: From document: RPG Character System (Fully Integrated into Weekly Modules) — chunk 112 (chunk 112/555)
